name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **CF INDUSTRIES -6.5% TO $119.76 / NUTRIEN -4.4% TO $73.81 — APRIL 17 PLAYBOOK RERUN: HORMUZ REOPENING SIGNAL = QATAR FERTILIZER NORMALIZATION EXPECTATION = SCARCITY-PREMIUM UNWIND.** MOU framework includes explicit Hormuz blockade lift → Qatar fertilizer/urea export normalization expectation. This is the SECOND-CLEANEST peace-dividend trade after energy. Today: same scarcity-premium unwind triggered by the same kind of news that drove CF -9.6% / NTR -5.2% on April 17 (when Iran first signaled Hormuz reopening). Urea generic at $577.50/T (May 5) drifting lower; Argus Q2 forecast $715 (Yuzhny FOB benchmark) has room to compress further; Egypt NCIC late-April spot $852/t represents the high-water mark; Yuzhny FOB benchmarks softening. CF is now -10.5% on the month and tracking the symmetrical inverse of the Hormuz-closure scarcity rally that drove it +27% in 3 months. **Reversibility:** if MOU signs, CF could give back another 8-12%; urea moderates toward Argus $715 forecast. If Iran rejects, CF bounces +6-9% within 48hr on premium re-pricing.
